title: blastus mollissimus
intro: Blastus mollissimus, also known as the woolly blastus or Indian mulberry, is a deciduous shrub native to tropical Asia. Its leaves and stems have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Blastus mollissimus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate skin conditions, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and poultices, and are often used to treat various ailments, including wounds, infections, and digestive issues.
